title: Chickpea Shawarma Bowls
description: A vibrant Mediterranean bowl featuring crispy roasted chickpeas seasoned with warm shawarma spices, fresh vegetables, creamy lemon tahini dressing, and all the classic Middle Eastern fixings. This nutritious and satisfying meal brings the bold flavors of shawarma to a wholesome grain bowl format.
ingredients: 1 (15 ounce) can chickpeas, rinsed and drained
1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, plus more to taste
3-4 cups cooked quinoa, rice, or cauliflower rice
1 cup halved cherry or grape tomatoes
1 cup pita chips (optional; use GF if needed)
1/2 cup tzatziki sauce, store-bought or homemade
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper for easier cleanup and better crisping.

Dry the chickpeas thoroughly by patting with paper towels or a clean kitchen towel — this is the key to getting them crispy. In a medium bowl, combine the chickpeas with 1 tablespoon olive oil, 1 teaspoon cumin, 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon smoked paprika, 1/2 teaspoon kosher salt, and 1/4 teaspoon turmeric. Stir until chickpeas are well coated.
Spread chickpeas in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet. Roast for 25-30 minutes, shaking the pan halfway through, until chickpeas are golden and crispy.
While chickpeas roast, make the lemon tahini dressing. In a small bowl or jar, whisk together 1/3 cup tahini, 1 minced clove garlic, 4 tablespoons lemon juice, and 1/3 cup hot water until smooth.

Season dressing with salt and pepper to taste. If the dressing is too thick, add water 1 tablespoon at a time and whisk again. Note that dressing will thicken as it sits — thin again before serving if needed.
Assemble the bowls by dividing 3-4 cups cooked quinoa, rice, or cauliflower rice among 4 bowls (about 3/4-1 cup per bowl).
Divide toppings among the 4 bowls: roasted chickpeas, Romaine lettuce, cherry tomatoes, cucumber, red onion, pita chips, a dollop of tzatziki sauce, Italian parsley, and crumbled feta cheese.
Drizzle generously with lemon tahini dressing and serve immediately.
notes: For gluten-free: use gluten-free pita chips or omit them entirely. All other ingredients are naturally gluten-free.

For meal prep, bowls can be prepped 3-4 days ahead. Store chickpeas, grains, and vegetables in containers in the fridge. Keep tahini dressing, tzatziki sauce, and pita chips separate until ready to eat to prevent sogginess.
For vegan: omit feta cheese and tzatziki sauce, or use dairy-free alternatives. Add extra tahini dressing for creaminess.
For a more authentic shawarma spice blend, add a pinch of ground cinnamon and ground coriander.
